name: Roasted Cornish Hens
recipeIngredient: 1 branch fresh rosemary, or 2 teaspoons dried
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 400°F.
2. Pat the hens dry with a clean cloth.
3. Squeeze the juice from one lemon into a medium bowl.
4. Stuff each hen cavity with half of the lemon, 2 cloves garlic, and 1/2 branch of fresh rosemary.
5. Mix together honey, olive oil, salt, and pepper in the bowl until blended with the lemon juice.
6. Baste the hens with the lemon honey sauce.
7. Place the hens in a small roasting pan.
8. Roast for 20 minutes at 400°F.
9. Reduce heat and continue to roast for an additional 20-30 minutes, basting occasionally.
10. Roast until juices run clear from the thigh.
11. Transfer hens to a cutting board and let sit for 10 minutes.
12. Place the roasting pan over a stove burner with low heat.
13. Stir in white wine and bring to a simmer.
14. Scrape brown bits from the bottom of the pan into the sauce.
15. Add chicken stock and continue to simmer.
16. Serve hens hot with the sauce.
